ch1.title: Chapter 1: The Beginning
ch1.text:
We had thought the battle was over, funnily, the debris from this broken city hurt more than the actual attack. Trudging street to street there was not a single place you couldn’t view burning with hot red flames, engulfing your heart for a place which was once so beautiful. But I suppose that was just what war was and did. Tore people apart.
But I had no time to even think about it. My clothes were torn and I had no choice but to limp through main street which was almost unrecognizable. Other than the flames of the two story lodges in front of me, my chest was engulfed with a vengeful spirit. If I ever came upon your vision as I had to the person I held on my back, you would be enlightened to my laborious breaths and crimson stained skin.
Fortunate you could call me, I was fortunate enough to not have a broken bone, or for random rebar to fall from the skies and impale me through the chest, such as my commander. It has to be worse that he was wide awake, braving the excruciating pain without a hitch as I would expect of a person like him.
His long black hair danced down my shoulders uncomfortable and sticky from a mix of blood and sweat. As implied, the metal through his chest had incapacitated him in regards to movement, yet somehow his mind was clear as day. Weakness was not something that ever reflected on his features even now was no different, his stubborn body was still able to convey necessary instructions for a soldier such as myself.
“Turn left then take a right on the next street.” He diligently guided me through the city, despite the cumbersome remains of the once beautiful urban masterpieces becoming obstacles in our way. Even with the clear street directions, it was hard to pursue when your body is constantly being attacked with hot and cold whenever you approach another pile of flaming trash or at worst, bodies.
The adrenaline of the apocalyptic scene was indescribable—explaining it to anybody but those who were there—devoid of better words its untranslatable. How did I walk through these swamps of streets, don’t ask me. Possession was the most likely option, by whom or what I have no clue. Maybe God or some sort of god was guiding me in this moment, not of my commander but possibly something else.
Its important to mention that a sizable amount of time has passed hitherto, the sun rising upon the horizon and my commander still alive and guiding me to what I’d like to hope is safety.
“With all due respect commander,” I took a deep breath. “Where are we?”
